Phillies vs Blue Jays Picks and Predictions: Philadelphia Leaves Canada With Win   The Toronto Blue Jays’ front office had seen enough. The team has underachieved to this point and Wednesday they decided a change needed to be made, firing manager Charlie Montoyo ahead of tonight’s game against the Philadelphia Phillies.

Stampeders vs Blue Bombers Week 6 Picks and Predictions: Winnipeg Remains Unbeaten   Week 6 in the CFL is headlined by a Friday night matchup between the Calgary Stampeders and Winnipeg Blue Bombers. These are the last two unbeaten teams in the CFL and oddsmakers expect the Stamps to lose their first game of the year, with CFL betting lines opening with the Bombers as 4-point home favorites.

Beginner's guide to betting on baseball   From picking which team will win to how many strikeouts a pitcher will have, there’s numerous ways to wager on baseball. If you’re new to betting on baseball, here’s what you need to know.

MLB Draft Odds: Prep Star Jones Clear Favorite to Go 1st Overall   The 2022 Major League Baseball draft will begin on Sunday, July 17, and betting markets are available. The biggest question on everyone’s mind is who will the Baltimore Orioles take with the top pick.

Open Championship 2022 Tiger Woods: Odds, Picks & Best Bets   Tiger Woods has arguably the most storied career in golf history and now he returns to the most historic course in golf, the Old Course at St. Andrews, as he’ll tee it up at the 150th British Open.

How a teen 'vampire's' blog blew up a murder trial — and prevented a wrongful conviction   The blog contained a description of her 'likes,' which included: 'Blood, pain ... rain, darkness, thunder ... fire, my bedroom, sex ... cemeteries, knives'
True Crime Byline: We should have seen JoAnn Wilson's murder coming   Leader-Post city editor Barb Pacholik recalls the many turns of the Colin Thatcher case across decades.

True Crime Byline: How one childhood incident pushed Dawn Crey into a downward spiral from which she would never escape   The new podcast True Crime Byline looks back at The Vancouver Sun investigation into Vancouver's missing women, a case that eventually led to the arrest and conviction of Robert Pickton. This profile of missing woman Dawn Crey was part of a series of investigative stories published in 2001.

True Crime Byline: A Politician Murdered his Ex Wife. We Should Have Seen it Coming   Looking back on the murder of JoAnn Wilson today, what's most surprising is that we were surprised it happened at all.
HAVE YOUR SAY: Answer our daily Toronto Sun poll question

FATAH: Time for Canada to end monopolies in telecom sector   It was a Black Friday for Canadians, but not the one we are used to. Not one filled with incredible sales and deals. In fact, the Black Friday of July 8 was truly black because of the crash of Rogers Internet services countrywide.

GOLDSTEIN: CBC sucks and blows on using the ’N-word'   In a head-spinning example of corporate confusion, inconsistency and hypocrisy, CBC-Radio Canada is defending its use of the ’N-word’ four times on air, after it effectively ended prominent CBC journalist Wendy Mesley’s career for using it twice off air.

LILLEY: Trudeau fiddles while economy burns amid rising inflation, interest rates   Prime Minister Justin Trudeau is failing Canada and Canadians in every way imaginable right now and he doesn’t seem to see any problem.

CHARLEBOIS AND TAYLOR: Dealing with 'Greedflation'   Accusations of gouging in the food industry have reached an all-time high. According to a recent survey, 68% of Canadians believe food corporations are taking advantage of the inflationary cycle to increase prices, and it’s not just in retail. While both Quebec and British Columbia now have class-action lawsuits against the beef industry, many trade groups and politicians are now asking the federal government to investigate.
'The Simpsons' star Yeardley Smith gets married years after chance meeting   Yeardley Smith has married a former detective she met at a Springfield event neither of them wanted to go to.
'Thor: Love and Thunder' post-credits scenes, explained   (Warning this story contains spoilers)
Studio 54 owner Mark Fleischman dies by assisted suicide   Ex Studio 54 owner Mark Fleischman has died by assisted suicide at Dignitas, according to his former business partner.
'Funny Girl' producers explain Beanie Feldstein's early exit from show   Beanie Feldstein “decided it was best for her” to leave Funny Girl early.

Tom Brady: Getting my kids to understand privilege is the 'hardest thing' about being a parent   Tom Brady says the “hardest thing” about being a parent is getting his children to understand they are privileged.
ASK AMY: Cousin's caregiving is actually care-taking   Dear Amy: My cousin was living with and taking care of my mom near the end of my mom’s life.

You say tomato - I say dessert!   With all the glorious summer weather we’ve been enjoying, many home vegetable gardens are already bursting with  nature’s goodness – from sweet tree fruits to zucchini running riot to all those charming, sweet cherry tomatoes that  are sprouting everywhere, including on people’s balconies.
Getting lost in the purple haze of lavender's magic   A celebration of Canada's lavender farms

Hit the road to see some spectacular Ontario spots   Should you stay or should you go?

SUNshine Girl Taylor   SUNshine Girl Taylor, our lovely Leo, is on the prowl again, preferably in a Tesla. Her dream job is to be a hairstylist and she likes watching The Breakfast Club, scrolling on social media and listening to Juice World. (Toronto Sun)

SUNshine Girl Nakitia   SUNshine Girl Nakitia must be looking forward to summer as she loves to spend time with family. The brown-eyed Sagittarius, an aspiring model, also likes to sing, play soccer and anything to do with animals. (STAN BEHAL/Toronto Sun/Postmedia Network)

SUNshine Girl Carmen   SUNshine Girl Carmen is a brown-eyed adventurer who loves travel and experiencing new cultures. She also enjoys shopping, eating, and music. Carmen worked at Disney, so naturally she a fan of all things Mickey. Some day, she hopes to work in California. (Ernest Doroszuk/Toronto Sun)
SUNshine Girl Jessica   SUNshine Girl Jessica is a blue-eyed Aquarius who loves cats, modelling, hockey, lacrosse, hunting — and fast cars. (Stan Behal/Toronto Sun)

SUNshine Girl Stephanie   SUNshine Girl Stephanie loves to spend time with family, preferably at the beach. The 5-foot-7, blue-eyed Aries, who first posed for us in July 2019, also loves painting, modelling and working out. (Stan Behal/Toronto Sun)

How do I know if have a sinus infection?   If you have blocked nose, or pressure around your face and eyes, bad breath or pain with chewing, you may have a sinus infection.

As Told To: 'Not everyone knows how to react to someone with a life-altering injury'   But you can start by not raising your voice or feeling the need to speak slower, says Russ Davies, who has been living with a brain injury since 2009: 'People think you aren't capable of having a conversation.'

What are the signs of cataracts?   While there is currently no known way to prevent cataracts, you can reduce your risk by wearing sunglasses, eating foods rich in antioxidants, and getting regular eye exams.

WATCH: #HowieMandel breaks TikTok with a photo of an anal prolapse   The America’s Got Talent judge took over TikTok last weekend by posting a photo of a severe anal prolapse — which aren't as rare as we'd like them to be.

Drinking alone when you're young linked to alcohol problems later in life   Researchers say young people often drink alone as a way to cope with negative emotions, a pattern of use that has been linked to alcohol use disorder.
Ontario driver found unconscious behind the wheel faces a number of drug charges   A 40-year-old man from Kenora was arrested following a tip from the public.
Ozzy Osbourne drops $1k at cannabis dispensary and then decides not to smoke   The Black Sabbath frontman has been sober for almost a decade.

Joe Rogan responds to border drug seizure: ‘You should take all these drugs and rethink your life’   Border agents seized small amounts of ecstasy, cannabis and psilocybin mushrooms from a vehicle entering New York.
Study claims regular cannabis use impairs performance in female athletes   While cannabis users produced significantly less power, they did not display as much anaerobic fatigue
Break-in report leads officers to 'hundreds of cannabis plants spread across multiple rooms'   The pot plants were taken away to be destroyed and the growing equipment was to be made useless.
